hard.competitor: Ivalua (incumbent, partial); 1-2 unnamed external S2P shortlist peers; internal ExAchat / do-nothing

north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is shortlisted (3-4 vendors) and product-preferred on intake, but engagement has thinned since the 10 Jun oral defense: Ubisoft paused for internal alignment (Frederic Hamelin, 24 Jun) before deployment talks, there is still no champion after 147 days, EB Franz Pinto and DM Anne-Sophie Dupont are mapped but unengaged, and the 30 Sep close date is not credible.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 05 Feb 2026 (151 days old as of 06 Jul), qualified 18 Feb. Stage Formal Evaluation; no submission-date fields populated, so time-in-stage is inferred from the Ubisoft RFP calendar (RFP issued 17 Apr, offers submitted 15 May, evaluation 05 Jun, oral defense 10 Jun, IT/integration meeting 19 Jun) = ~57 days into post-shortlist evaluation. Close date 30 Sep is 86 days out. Last Salesforce activity 01 Jul (Pierre sent DocuSign connector info, awaiting reply). Remaining path per Next_Step and RFP timeline: confirm preferred-vendor status, scope the short POC (was due 25 Jun, now ~11 days overdue), SoW workshop mid-Jul (contingent on preferred confirmation), Ubisoft feedback Jul, commercials, EB/SVP-Finance sign-off, contracting/InfoSec/legal. That is 4-5 months of work compressed into 86 days. No slips are recorded in field history yet, but current pace points to a Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 close (3-5 month slip likely).
evidence: No champion after 151 days (Next_Step 15 Jun, 04 Jun, 21 May: 'Still no champion', 'Need to find a champion', 'Still missing a champion'). Frederic Hamelin is warming (accepted LinkedIn, 15 Jun; confirmed the pause is routine, 24 Jun) but is not advocating or feeding competitive intel.

Buyer-initiated pause: Frederic Hamelin, 24 Jun email: 'we first need to finalise an internal alignment before engaging in discussions on deployment and integration.' Pierre has been chasing Said for a call since 23 Jun with no substantive reply.

EB Franz Pinto (Group Procurement Director) surfaced only at the 10 Jun oral defense, has had no 1:1, and did not accept the LinkedIn connect (Next_Step 15 Jun); DM Anne-Sophie Dupont unengaged, management outreach from the owner's manager got no feedback (Next_Step 21 May). Real budget authority sits with an unnamed SVP Finance (Corentin, 09 Feb: the direction call routes via 'notre SVP finance').
Short POC scope overdue: due 25 Jun per Next_Step 24 Jun, still not confirmed. The POC is the gate to preferred-vendor status and the mid-Jul SoW workshop.
Competitive field is blind: shortlist of 3-4 with 1-2 unnamed external S2P rivals Ubisoft is 'still hesitating on' (Next_Step 01 Jun); no champion to name them.

Funding risk: Gabriel Hide, 09 Feb: 'vous etes au courant de la situation aussi du groupe Ubisoft qui n'est pas la meilleure actuellement... vous n'etes pas priorite'; a live 'keep and optimise internal' scenario (Corentin, 05 Feb) is the do-nothing threat.
Best Case is indefensible: no champion, EB/DM unengaged, blind competitive landscape, overdue POC, non-credible date. Recommend Pipeline until the POC lands and a champion is secured.

metrics.narrative: Ubisoft has articulated the pain sharply but never quantified it. Corentin Sebe framed the driver on 05 Feb: making requesters autonomous on low-value requests is '70 to 80 percent of our success factor', with better contract-consumption tracking a secondary goal. Gabriel Hide sharpened it on 09 Feb into a transformation, not continuous improvement: 'on a reellement un sujet de fracture ou de rupture... retirer les achats d'un tres grand nombre de transactions'. No buyer has stated current-state cost, cycle time, headcount, or a savings target. Zycus floated an ROI illustration on 09 Feb (20 percent additional spend under control, 2-3 percent savings on ~5M EUR), but that is rep-stated, not buyer-owned. Quantifying this with their finance lead ahead of the SVP-Finance presentation is the move.

economic_buyer.narrative: Franz Pinto (Group Procurement Director / Directeur des Achats Groupe) is the named economic buyer per Next_Step and the contact roles, but he is passive: he appeared only at the 10 Jun oral defense (his first appearance), has had no 1:1, and did not accept the LinkedIn connect (Next_Step 15 Jun). The evidence shows the ultimate budget call sits ABOVE him with an unnamed SVP Finance who gives 'une intonation sur une direction a prendre en fonction aussi d'autres caracteristiques financieres' (Corentin, 09 Feb), and Gabriel Hide noted the decision is 'above our paygrade... decide par la SVP' (09 Feb). Anne-Sophie Dupont is the DM/sponsor. No commercial or pricing conversation has happened with any of them, so budget authority is mapped but unvalidated. Move: secure a 1:1 with Franz Pinto (and, through him, the SVP Finance) to validate budget before commercials.
sources: Avoma call 09 Feb 2026 (Corentin Sebe, Gabriel Hide)

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are well-evidenced and favour Zycus. Primary: non-buyer user adoption and requester autonomy via a natural-language front door in Microsoft Teams (Corentin, 05 Feb: autonomy is '70-80 percent of our success factor'; Gabriel, 09 Feb, endorsed the Teams-embedded Merlin approach as 'tres interessant... plutot que de demander deja a entrer dans une plateforme'). Secondary confirmed criteria surfaced on the 09 Feb demo: an isolated savings-declaration module (Corentin asked for iSave to work 'completement autonome... isole du reste'), supplier onboarding/risk, contract repository, and native (not acquisition-assembled) AI. Explicitly de-prioritised: e-sourcing ('ce n'est pas un axe prioritaire', Gabriel 09 Feb) and heavy contract authoring (legal teams are external). The RFP is 'very intake oriented' (Next_Step 04 Jun), which plays to Zycus's intake strength.
sources: Avoma demo call 09 Feb 2026 (Corentin Sebe, Gabriel Hide)

decision_process.narrative: Ubisoft is running a formal, self-directed RFP and following it strictly since the RFI. The published calendar (Next_Step 04 Jun): RFP issued 17 Apr, bidder questions 30 Apr, offers 15 May, evaluation 05 Jun, demos June, Ubisoft feedback July. The process map read from the call/activity sequence: operational project leads (Corentin Sebe, Gabriel Hide) ran discovery and the demo; the 10 Jun oral defense pulled in EB Franz Pinto for the first time; a 19 Jun technical/integration meeting engaged Ubisoft IT (Sofian Moraine, Said). The decision then routes internally to the SVP Finance/management, who own the go/no-go across four scenarios (keep internal, optimise internal, partial replace, full replace). The current gate is an internal-alignment step (Frederic Hamelin, 24 Jun) that must clear before deployment discussions resume.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible yet, which is normal at Formal Evaluation. Per the Next_Step plan, paper only starts if Zycus is confirmed preferred: SoW workshop mid-Jul, then commercials. Two structural notes already shape it: Ubisoft's legal teams are external with their own tooling ('equipes Legales qui sont plutot externes', Corentin 09 Feb), which will complicate any MSA/SOW routing; and a DocuSign integration question is already live (Said, answered by Pierre 01 Jul with the plug-and-play connector). Current-contract expiration is not on record. Visibility is created by getting the SoW workshop booked once preferred-vendor status is confirmed.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owner-attributed. Ubisoft's fragmented ecosystem (internal ExAchat for project/contract/supplier management, PeopleSoft and Ivalua across regions, e-sourcing run over email) drives maverick spend and low policy compliance. Gabriel Hide, 09 Feb, called it a 'sujet de fracture ou de rupture', with a real objective 'sur les mois ou l'annee a venir de retirer les achats d'un tres grand nombre de transactions' by making non-buyer requesters autonomous. Corentin Sebe owns the operational tooling review; the pain owner above them is the procurement management / SVP Finance driving the target operating model. The inferred but unquantified risk is the cost and compliance exposure of leaving it unaddressed (do-nothing scenario).

champion.narrative: No champion after 151 days (Next_Step 15 Jun, 04 Jun, 21 May explicitly: 'Still no champion' / 'Need to find a champion' / 'Still missing a champion'). Frederic Hamelin (HQ Procurement Director) is the closest and is developing: he accepted the LinkedIn connect (15 Jun) and reassured Zycus the pause is only internal alignment 'aucune alerte specifique' (24 Jun) - but he is not selling Zycus internally, not sharing competitive intel, and not driving the agenda. Corentin Sebe and Gabriel Hide are engaged operationally and gave positive feedback ('hyper interessant', Gabriel 09 Feb) but explicitly stay neutral evaluators ('je ne peux rien promettre', Gabriel 09 Feb). What is still missing: an internal advocate with access to Franz Pinto / the SVP Finance who will carry the Zycus case. Converting Frederic is the highest-leverage next move.

competition.narrative: Zycus is shortlisted to 3-4 vendors and was sent the invitation before the shortlist was even finalised, which signals a product-level preference on intake ('on peut estimer que sur un point de vue produit on est prefere', Next_Step 04 Jun). The strongest current threat is inertia: a live 'keep and optimise the internal stack' scenario (ExAchat + regional Ivalua/PeopleSoft) that Corentin listed first among four options (05 Feb), amplified by Ubisoft's group financial strain and 'vous n'etes pas priorite' (Gabriel, 09 Feb). Ivalua is the incumbent, already contracted and partially deployed on the French market and used in some entities including Canada, but never rolled out globally. There are 1-2 unnamed external S2P rivals still in the shortlist that Ubisoft is 'still hesitating on' (01 Jun) - the key blind spot. The wedge is native, single-codebase AI (Merlin) embedded in Teams for user adoption, versus acquisition-assembled suites.

deal_movement.summary: Active through the RFP but cooling post-defense: after the 10 Jun oral defense and 19 Jun IT/integration meeting, Ubisoft paused for internal alignment (24 Jun) and Pierre's 23 Jun-01 Jul chase for a follow-up call has gone largely unanswered. Forecast was lifted Pipeline to Best Case on 01 Jun.

items.change: Amount raised 150k to 250k EUR after the demo
Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation
RFP response submitted via shared Showpad workspace
Forecast category moved Pipeline to Best Case
Technical/integration follow-up meeting held with Ubisoft IT (Sofian, Said, Brice, Neeraj, Varun)
Frederic Hamelin confirmed the delay is routine internal alignment, 'aucune alerte specifique'
Pierre sent DocuSign plug-and-play connector info to Said, awaiting reply

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is shortlisted (3-4) and product-preferred on intake, invited before the list was finalised. Ranked by CURRENT threat: (1) do-nothing / optimise-internal is the strongest threat, driven by Ubisoft's group financial strain and 'you're not a priority' (09 Feb); (2) 1-2 unnamed external S2P shortlist peers Ubisoft is 'still hesitating on' (01 Jun) - the live blind spot; (3) Ivalua, incumbent partially deployed on the French market / Canada but never global. Wedge across all three: native single-codebase Merlin AI embedded in Teams for user adoption.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / optimise internal (ExAchat + regional Ivalua/PeopleSoft)
competitors.quote: vous n'etes pas priorite... la situation du groupe Ubisoft qui n'est pas la meilleure actuellement; scenario 1: on garde notre existant et on ameliore
competitors.source: Avoma call 09 Feb 2026 (Gabriel Hide) + 05 Feb 2026 (Corentin Sebe)
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe status quo as compounding maverick-spend and compliance cost; land a short, cheap POC that proves Teams-embedded intake adoption the internal build cannot match.

competitors.name: Unnamed external S2P shortlist peers (1-2 vendors)
competitors.quote: Ubisoft we are shortlisted (3 or 4)... they are still hesitating on some of our competitors
competitors.how_we_win: Secure a champion (Frederic Hamelin) to name the peers, then differentiate on native agentic AI (Merlin autonomous negotiation) and single-database supplier model.

competitors.name: Ivalua (incumbent, partial deployment)
competitors.quote: on a du Evalua pour gerer sur certaines entites, notamment au Canada... deja pris un contrat avec un fournisseur de source de paie connu sur le marche francais, pas deploye au niveau global
competitors.how_we_win: Position unified single-codebase suite against Ivalua's fragmented regional footprint; win on user adoption and end-to-end AI orchestration.

competitors.name: ExAchat (internal build)
competitors.quote: un developpement interne qui vise a couvrir gestion de projet, repository contrat, gestion fournisseur prospects dans un outil unique
competitors.how_we_win: Show the internal tool cannot deliver agentic automation or non-buyer adoption at scale; make the Teams/Merlin demo the proof point.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba / Ivalua (acquisition-suite reference)
competitors.quote: Competing S2P platforms often result from acquisitions causing integration and maintenance challenges
competitors.source: Avoma call 09 Feb 2026 (rep positioning only - not buyer-named in the eval)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with 100 percent organically-developed single database and true SaaS (no re-implementation on upgrade).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the buyer's stated priorities (requester autonomy, Teams-embedded AI, native single-suite), with two neutral/de-prioritised areas (e-sourcing, contract authoring) that are non-blocking.

items.criterion: Non-buyer user adoption / requester autonomy via natural-language front door
items.quote: arriver a faire en sorte que les demandeurs soient plus autonomes... 70-80 pour cent de notre facteur de reussite

items.criterion: AI embedded in Microsoft Teams rather than a separate platform
items.quote: plutot interessant de pouvoir passer directement par l'outil collaboratif que tout le monde utilise
on avait en tete le diagramme... une offre de modules qui circule autour de votre modele IA... c'est bien ce qu'on avait en tete
il nous faudrait juste un module completement isole du reste... on comprend que c'est possible dans votre module (iSave)
le e-sourcing chez Ubisoft se fait par mail... ce n'est pas un axe prioritaire de notre direction

items.requirement: Hold a deployment/integration meeting with Zycus IT experts
items.quote: we would like to arrange a follow-up meeting with your IT team... please share your available time slots

items.requirement: Demo covering end-to-end intake-to-invoice flow and their specific use cases
items.quote: some specific points we would like to cover... 1. General end-to-end flow from Intake to Invoicing

items.requirement: Clarify DocuSign integration for the S2P scope
items.quote: you had questions regarding Docusign Integration, please find information about our plug and play connector

items.deliverable: Complete internal alignment and confirm whether Zycus is the preferred vendor
items.grounding_quote: we first need to finalise an internal alignment before engaging in discussions on deployment and integration

items.deliverable: Confirm a date for the technical/deployment meeting (early Jul TBC)
items.grounding_quote: NS Technical meeting (early July TBC) If we are preferred then SoW Workshop by mid of July

items.area: Competitive intel on the 1-2 unnamed external S2P shortlist peers
items.quote: they are still hesitating on some of our competitors

items.area: Quantified business case / ROI (no savings, cycle-time, or headcount figures from the buyer)
items.quote: no buyer quote quantifies the current-state cost or target savings

items.area: Ultimate budget authority above Franz Pinto (unnamed SVP Finance)
items.quote: decide par la SVP... above our paygrade
Still no champion / Need to find a champion

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a shortlisted-but-championless deal against a strong do-nothing threat.
flags: Convert Frederic Hamelin from warm influencer to champion: he accepted the LinkedIn connect and defends the timeline, but you need him naming the rival peers and carrying Zycus to Franz Pinto.

Break the intake blind spot with a small, fast POC: the buyer treats the POC as the preferred-vendor gate; scoping it now neutralises the 'optimise internal' do-nothing scenario before the SVP-Finance review.
Multi-thread to power before commercials: EB Franz Pinto has only appeared once and the real budget call sits with an unnamed SVP Finance - secure exec-to-buyer access and validate budget early.

items.name: Franz Pinto
items.title: Group Procurement Director / Directeur des Achats Groupe
items.risk: Passive. Surfaced only at the 10 Jun oral defense, no 1:1, did not accept the LinkedIn connect; budget authority unvalidated and the real call sits with an unnamed SVP Finance above him.

items.name: Anne-Sophie Dupont
items.title: Financial Planning & Procurement Operational Excellence Director
items.risk: Unengaged. Named sponsor / head of procurement excellence; VP-Sales and manager outreach both got no feedback (Next_Step 21 May).

items.name: Frederic Hamelin
items.title: HQ Procurement Director
items.risk: Developing, not yet champion. Accepted LinkedIn (15 Jun), reassured on the pause (24 Jun), but not advocating or sharing competitive intel.

items.name: Corentin Sebe
items.title: Procurement Project Manager
items.risk: Owns the tooling review; engaged operationally but a neutral evaluator.

items.name: Gabriel Hide
items.risk: Procurement project/process team, P2P focus; gave positive demo feedback but explicitly non-committal ('je ne peux rien promettre').

items.name: Sofian Moraine
items.risk: RFP orchestrator running demo/meeting logistics; responsive but OOO windows (10-26 Jun) slow cadence. No SF title on record.

champion_strength.summary: No champion after 151 days. Frederic Hamelin is the only developing candidate (accepted LinkedIn 15 Jun, defended the timeline 24 Jun) but is not advocating or feeding competitive intel; Corentin Sebe and Gabriel Hide are neutral evaluators. Without a champion, Zycus is blind to the 1-2 unnamed shortlist peers and cannot reach EB Franz Pinto or the SVP Finance.
champion_strength.source: Next_Step 15 Jun / 04 Jun / 21 May 2026; SF email Frederic Hamelin 24 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is the buyer's explicit lens and Zycus's Merlin story landed: Gabriel Hide confirmed the modules-around-an-AI-core diagram matched their mental model (09 Feb) and called the autonomous-negotiation agent 'impressionnant'. Positioning is strong and on-target, not under-played.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 8 / Strong is corroborated by the buyer's own words, not just rep framing: Ubisoft wants to 'unify AI across modules to avoid fragmented quick wins' and embed procurement in Teams via an AI chatbot. AIS is not over-rated; the only cap is that no formal AI POC/RFP mandate is signed yet.

items.detail: No champion after 151 days; EB Franz Pinto passive and DM Anne-Sophie Dupont unengaged, so Zycus cannot navigate post-shortlist internal politics.
Ubisoft's group financial strain and explicit 'you are not a priority' put funding at risk; a live 'optimise internal / do-nothing' scenario competes for the budget.
30 Sep close is not credible: 4-5 months of remaining work (POC, SoW, commercials, EB sign-off, contracting/InfoSec) compressed into 86 days, with an internal-alignment pause already in play.
Real budget authority sits with an unnamed SVP Finance above Franz Pinto; Zycus has no access to that person before commercials.
items.source: Avoma call 09 Feb 2026 (Corentin Sebe, Gabriel Hide)

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling: post-defense the buyer paused for internal alignment (24 Jun) and Pierre's 23 Jun-01 Jul chase for a call has gone largely unanswered; engagement is thinning even though the deal is live.
items: Buyer pause for internal alignment before deployment talks (24 Jun)
Rep chasing Said for a call since 23 Jun with no substantive reply
Key stakeholders OOO (Sofian 10-26 Jun) slowing cadence

items.action: Scope and propose the short POC with Frederic Hamelin to lock preferred-vendor status.
items.trigger: POC scope due 25 Jun, still not confirmed; it gates the mid-Jul SoW workshop
items.expected_effect: Converts product preference into the preferred-vendor gate and unblocks the SoW workshop, defending against the do-nothing scenario.

items.action: Ask Frederic Hamelin to name the remaining shortlist vendors and sponsor Zycus internally.
items.trigger: Shortlist of 3-4 with 1-2 unnamed peers Ubisoft is 'still hesitating on'
items.expected_effect: Removes the competitive blind spot and begins building the missing champion.

items.action: Confirm the early-July technical/deployment meeting and close the DocuSign integration thread with Said.
items.trigger: Technical meeting early Jul TBC; DocuSign connector info sent 01 Jul awaiting reply
items.expected_effect: Restarts cadence after the internal-alignment pause and keeps integration confidence high.

items.action: Secure a 1:1 with Franz Pinto to validate budget and reach the SVP Finance.
items.trigger: EB appeared only once (10 Jun oral defense), no 1:1, LinkedIn connect declined
items.expected_effect: Maps and engages true budget authority before commercials, making the forecast defensible.

items.action: Deliver an ROI model quantifying intake-autonomy savings for the SVP-Finance review.
items.trigger: Pain is confirmed but never quantified; buyer preparing a management/SVP-Finance presentation
items.expected_effect: Turns qualitative pain into a funded business case and counters the internal-optimisation option.

critical_signals.text: Zycus was shortlisted to 3-4 and invited before the list was finalised on intake strength, but is still blind to the 1-2 external rivals Ubisoft is hesitating on (01 Jun).
Ubisoft's group financial troubles and an explicit 'you are not a priority' keep a low-cost optimise-internal option alive as the biggest threat to funding.
The 10 Jun oral defense and 19 Jun IT/integration meeting landed, but Ubisoft then paused for internal alignment before any deployment talks (24 Jun).
Economic buyer Franz Pinto surfaced at the 10 Jun defense but has had no 1:1 and declined the LinkedIn connect, while decision-maker Anne-Sophie Dupont stays unengaged.
The buyer asked for a standalone, fully isolated savings-declaration module (iSave) and confirmed e-sourcing is out of scope (09 Feb).

customer_preference.evidence: Invited before the shortlist was finalised and RFP is intake-centric ('sur un point de vue produit on est prefere', 04 Jun); Gabriel Hide called the demo 'hyper interessant' - positive but non-committal, no decisive lean.

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (requester autonomy = '70-80 percent of our success factor', a 'sujet de rupture' to remove buyers from many transactions) but no buyer-stated savings, cycle-time, or headcount figures.

scope_change.detail: Amount raised from 150k to 250k EUR on 17 Mar after the demo; product scope is the full AI-bundled S2P suite (iContract, iSupplier, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk, Merlin, Merlin Intake), not narrowed.

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120d (Mar-Jul 2026); field history to Mar 2026
gaps: Both matched Avoma calls are from Feb 2026 (05 Feb, 09 Feb); no transcribed buyer call since, so the post-shortlist read (Jun) rests on the email/Next_Step trail.
Identity of the 1-2 unnamed external S2P shortlist competitors is unknown.
No quantified ROI / savings figures from the buyer.
